rule: The library enables creating formatted console tables from enumerable collections, POCO data, or via a fluent interface for manual configuration of headers and rows.
Visual output is customizable, supporting numeric alignment and various pre-defined formats such as Default, Alternative, and Minimal styles.
Table schema integrity is enforced by requiring column definitions prior to row insertion and ensuring that every row contains the exact number of values specified by the columns.

Table rendering and layout are governed by configuration and content-aware logic, encompassing automatic numeric alignment, word-wrapping at word break delimiters, Unicode-accurate width calculations, and optional metadata display such as total row counts.
Tabular data output can be configured to right-align numeric values and toggle the visibility of row count summaries.
Data structures formatted as dictionaries can be automatically converted into tabular representations where keys correspond to column or row headers.
Table formatting must account for the visual display width of Unicode characters and support configurable column alignment (e.g., numeric right-alignment) to ensure visual consistency.
The library must support data ingestion from multiple standard .NET structures including IEnumerable, IDictionary, and DataTable.
The system must support multiple rendering formats (e.g., boxed, Markdown, minimal) and allow output redirection to any TextWriter, defaulting to the standard console output.
